one_liner: Twenty is an open-source CRM offering full REST/GraphQL API access via API keys, OAuth2 (with dynamic client registration + PKCE), or JWT, self-serve on both free self-hosted and paid cloud plans.

auth_methods.notes: Created via Settings > API & Webhooks; used as Bearer token; full workspace access.
Authorization code + PKCE for user-facing apps, client credentials for server-to-server; dynamic client registration per RFC 7591 (no manual dashboard setup).
JWT tokens for session-based web app auth; personal access tokens also available for long-lived personal use.

self_serve_notes: Free self-hosted version includes full Pro-tier REST/GraphQL API access with no restrictions. On managed cloud, API access requires Pro plan starting at $9/user/month; no separate API-only tier. API keys and OAuth client registration are fully self-serve (no approval process needed).

api_surface.notes: Both REST and GraphQL APIs are available and fully documented, plus a JavaScript SDK. Custom objects/fields are automatically exposed via the API.

evidence.supports: Details API key, OAuth, JWT, and personal access token authentication methods with examples.
Describes OAuth2 authorization code+PKCE and client credentials flows, dynamic client registration via RFC 7591, and scopes.
Confirms Pro plan at $9/user/month includes API access; self-hosted free tier includes full API access.

flags: Multiple third-party MCP server implementations exist (mhenry3164, studio7A, Praeses0 fork) but none appear to be an official first-party Twenty MCP server.
Self-hosted vs cloud API parity should be double-checked for edge cases (e.g., OAuth dynamic registration availability on self-hosted instances).
